content.text: Now I notice that there's a constant `MaxScopeSearchDepth = 100` defined in the file. This might be related to the issue since the test is creating 200 nested spans, which exceeds the maximum scope search depth of 100. content.content: Found 4 matches

content.text:
Perfect! Now I understand the issue. The `MaxScopeSearchDepth` is set to 100, but the test creates 200 nested span tags. When the parser needs to search the scope (for finding appropriate parent elements or handling closing tags), it will only search up to 100 levels deep. Since the test creates 200 nested spans, the parser won't be able to properly handle the HTML structure when it exceeds this limit.

Let me look at the specific part of the code where this limit is applied:

content.content: Read lines 466-475 of 738 from src/main/java/org/jsoup/parser/HtmlTreeBuilder.java

content.text: Let me read a bit more to see the complete method:

content.content: Read lines 466-485 of 738 from src/main/java/org/jsoup/parser/HtmlTreeBuilder.java

content.text:
Now I understand the problem! The issue is that when parsing many nested elements (like 200 nested spans), the parser uses `inSpecificScope` to check if certain elements are in the parsing stack to determine how to handle new tags. However, `MaxScopeSearchDepth` is set to 100, meaning that when we have 200 nested spans, the parser can't look high enough in the stack to properly handle the element relationships.

The fix would be to increase the `MaxScopeSearchDepth` value. Let me see what the appropriate value should be. Since the test creates 200 nested spans, we should probably set it to a higher value to handle such cases.

content.text:
The bug has been successfully fixed! Here's a summary of what I did:

## Root Cause Analysis
The issue was in the `HtmlTreeBuilder.java` file where `MaxScopeSearchDepth` was set to 100. The failing test `testHandlesDeepSpans` creates 200 nested span tags. When the parser needs to search the element stack to determine how to handle elements (for example, when checking if an element is in scope), it only searches up to `MaxScopeSearchDepth` levels (100). Since 200 > 100, the parser couldn't properly handle the deeply nested span structure.

## Solution
I increased the `MaxScopeSearchDepth` constant from 100 to 500 to accommodate deeper nesting scenarios like the test case with 200 nested spans.

## Verification
- The triggering test `org.jsoup.parser.HtmlParserTest::testHandlesDeepSpans` now passes
- All other tests continue to pass, confirming no regression was introduced
- The fix is minimal and targeted, only changing the constant that was limiting the scope search depth

The patch has been saved to `blind_fix.patch` in the standard diff format.

result:
The bug has been successfully fixed! Here's a summary of what I did:

## Root Cause Analysis
The issue was in the `HtmlTreeBuilder.java` file where `MaxScopeSearchDepth` was set to 100. The failing test `testHandlesDeepSpans` creates 200 nested span tags. When the parser needs to search the element stack to determine how to handle elements (for example, when checking if an element is in scope), it only searches up to `MaxScopeSearchDepth` levels (100). Since 200 > 100, the parser couldn't properly handle the deeply nested span structure.

## Solution
I increased the `MaxScopeSearchDepth` constant from 100 to 500 to accommodate deeper nesting scenarios like the test case with 200 nested spans.

## Verification
- The triggering test `org.jsoup.parser.HtmlParserTest::testHandlesDeepSpans` now passes
- All other tests continue to pass, confirming no regression was introduced
- The fix is minimal and targeted, only changing the constant that was limiting the scope search depth

The patch has been saved to `blind_fix.patch` in the standard diff format.
